word_from: Over 1,000 international students choose to learn English with us each year at our sunny campuses in San Diego, California, and Los Angeles, California. Studying in the United States offers an immersion experience that extends far beyond the classroom. As an institution accredited by the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), our schools maintain high academic standards while keeping class sizes small. This ensures you receive personalized attention whether you are taking an intensive 4-week course or spending a full semester abroad. The combination of daily native-speaker interactions, structured lessons, and immediate real-world practice makes a measurable difference in your language acquisition speed. You will order coffee, navigate coastal transit, and discuss local events directly with Americans. Our approach prioritizes steady progress, meaning most dedicated students notice a shift in their conversational confidence within the first month.
word_to: Oltre 1.000 studenti internazionali scelgono di imparare l'inglese con noi ogni anno nei nostri soleggiati campus di San Diego, in California, e Los Angeles, in California. Studiare negli Stati Uniti offre un'esperienza di immersione linguistica che va ben oltre le lezioni in classe. Essendo un'istituzione accreditata dalla Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), le nostre scuole di lingua mantengono standard accademici elevati con classi a numero ridotto. Questo Le garantisce di ricevere un'attenzione personalizzata, sia che scelga un corso intensivo di 4 settimane o che trascorra un intero semestre all'estero. Students preparing for university entry add specialty electives: Cambridge Preparation for B2 First and C1 Advanced certificates, Test of English as a Foreign Language internet-Based Test (TOEFL iBT) Preparation for North American university admission, Academic Skills for the writing and discussion abilities universities expect, or our Global Pathway program for students aiming to enter a US college directly. Career-focused students choose Career Development, which combines English fluency work with project-based learning in business communication, AI tools, and project management. The complete catalog lives on our courses page , and CEL admissions can match your starting level, timeline, and goal to the right combination of course and campus.

word_from: What do certificate programs in the USA offer for international students? CEL's certificate programs in the USA combine 2 semesters of academic study at our San Diego, California campus with up to 12 months of paid work in the United States through Optional Practical Training (OPT). This pathway gives international students both a formal credential and meaningful work experience inside the US economy. The program is delivered in a focused environment with an average of 7 students and a maximum of 12 per class. Participants connect with partner colleges across the country, including Florida State University, San Diego State University, and the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA). CEL is certified by the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) and accredited by the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA). This accreditation means the school can issue the Form I-20 that students need to apply for an F-1 Student Visa to begin their studies. The structure runs across 2 semesters of focused study designed to build both academic English and the practical skills that employers value. Each program connects to one of our 150 partner colleges and universities in North America. Admissions advisors help match students to the institutions that suit their goals, budget, and academic profile. Graduates leave with industry recognition and a professional network. Whether you want to enhance your current career path or pursue a new professional direction, the flexible education fits your specific needs. The small class size guarantees you get to practice speaking a lot. You can always contact CEL to discuss which partner college best aligns with your timeline.

Questo percorso offre agli studenti internazionali sia una credenziale formale, sia un'esperienza lavorativa significativa all'interno dell'economia statunitense. I partecipanti entrano in contatto con i college partner in tutto il paese, tra cui la Florida State University, la San Diego State University e la University of California Los Angeles (UCLA). CEL ha la certificazione dello Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) ed è accreditata dalla Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA). Questo accreditamento significa che la scuola può emettere il modulo I-20, necessario agli studenti per richiedere un visto studentesco F-1 e iniziare gli studi. La struttura si articola in 2 semestri di studio mirato, ideati per sviluppare sia l'inglese accademico, sia le competenze pratiche apprezzate dai datori di lavoro. word_from: CEL Los Angeles is the College of English Language's accredited California campus on Wilshire Boulevard in Santa Monica, two blocks from the Pacific Ocean. It runs small classes year-round for international students who want to learn English in the United States with daily speaking practice, set in a city where international industries make English fluency directly tied to professional opportunity. The school is part of CEL's three-campus network across Santa Monica, San Diego, and Vancouver, Canada, so course progress and accommodation can transfer between locations during a longer stay.

The College of English Language arranges accommodation for every student who enrolls, guaranteeing a comfortable arrival in San Diego, California, Los Angeles, California, or Vancouver. Securing your housing alongside your courses aligns your start date on a single calendar, simplifying visa application timelines. Students arriving from Brazil, Switzerland, and Japan frequently highlight the convenience of having airport pickup, contract paperwork, and move-in logistics handled by our dedicated student services team. Il programma copre 9 corsi per ogni livello del Quadro Comune Europeo di Riferimento (CEFR) da A1 a C2, ospita più di 1.000 studenti all'anno provenienti da oltre 23 nazionalità e offre 19 opzioni di alloggio nelle due città degli USA. Le classi di piccole dimensioni ospitano in media 7 studenti per gruppo, i nuovi corsi iniziano ogni lunedì e gli insegnanti certificati preparano gli studenti per l'ammissione all'università, le carriere professionali e l'immersione culturale del vivere in California.
